name: Umm Wahb
title: Lioness of Ashura
shortDescription: The brave wife of Abdullah ibn Umayr who attacked the enemy with a tent pole and became the only female martyr of Ashura.
fullStory:
Umm Wahb bint Abd was the brave and faithful wife of Abdullah ibn Umayr al-Kalbi. She was a woman from the Namir ibn Qasit tribe who, when her husband decided to go to Karbala to help the son of the Prophet (PBUH), not only didn't stop him but asked him to take her along. Her presence in Karbala demonstrates the spiritual greatness and bravery of women in the epic of Ashura.

On the day of Ashura, when her husband Abdullah killed two warriors of the Kufan army, Umm Wahb, thrilled by this bravery, grabbed a club (or a tent pole) and ran towards the battlefield. She turned to her husband and said: 'May my parents be sacrificed for you! Fight to defend the pure ones of Muhammad's (PBUH) household'.

Abdullah tried to return her to the women's tent, but Umm Wahb pulled her husband's clothes and pleaded: 'I will not let you go until I am killed alongside you!'. Imam Hussain (AS), seeing this scene, sent her back to the women's tent and said: 'May God reward your family well, return to the tent, for Jihad is not obligatory upon women'. Umm Wahb obeyed and returned.

Hours later, when Abdullah ibn Umayr was martyred after a heroic battle, Umm Wahb came out of the tent, sat by the bloody body of her husband, wiped the dust from his face, and said: 'May Paradise be pleasant for you! I ask the God who granted you Paradise to place me alongside you'.

At that moment, Shimr ibn Dhi al-Jawshan, witnessing this loving and epic scene, ordered his slave (Rustam) to strike Umm Wahb's head with an iron mace. The slave struck such a blow to the head of this lioness that she passed away next to her husband's body, recording the honor of being 'the only female martyr of Karbala' in history.
quoteArabic: هَنيئاً لَكَ الجَنَّةُ! أَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ الَّذي رَزَقَكَ الجَنَّةَ أَنْ يَصْحَبَني مَعَكَ
quote: May Paradise be pleasant for you! I ask God who granted you Paradise to accompany me with you.
